notes: Played through entire game without any issues
title: Burly Men at Sea

notes:
To get it running at a reasonable speed I had to lower the Anti Aliasing settings, which normally require you to start the game while holding the Shift key.  This did not work, the settings dialog did not come up when starting while holding any combination of modifier keys.  I had to run with the '-show-screen-selector' command line argument / Launch Option so that I could turn down the anti-aliasing setting.

After these tweaks I was able to play the entire game and get every achievement.
title: Burly Men at Sea

notes: Trying to boot give a WINE error dialog that reads "Fatal error in gc: GetThreadContext failed". Clicking OK pops up a window with a progress bar that may be an error reporter, then exits out completely once it fills. Tried disabling esync, disabling D3d11, and using WINE D3d11, but none worked.
title: Burly Men at Sea

notes: Vastly improved this time compared to my earlier "Borked" report. Managed two play-throughs with no issues. I suspect that either there was a problem with the Proton instance initialization, in which case uninstalling and reinstalling would have fixed it, or there was some sort of graphics driver compatibility issue, in which case the driver update fixed it.
title: Burly Men at Sea
